description: Streaming UTF-8 decoder that handles split buffers, invalid sequences, overlong encodings, and surrogate codepoints per the W3C/WHATWG replacement policy. Emits (rune, width) pairs through a callback, where width is 0 for combining/zero-width codepoints, 1 for narrow, and 2 for east-asian-wide or emoji — suitable for terminal grid layout. Pure, no I/O, no dependencies.

library_notes.general: Single responsibility - one widget does one thing. No global state. No project-specific code, hardcoded paths, credentials, or environment-specific values. Expose variation points as constructor parameters or function arguments - no hardcoded thresholds, colors, sizes, or behavior flags. URLs and endpoints belong in examples as demonstration data, not in src/ - if a widget needs a URL to operate, it must be a parameter the caller provides. All dependencies must be declared in widget.json tech_stack.dependencies. Widgets cannot depend on other widgets - they must be fully self-contained. If you need logic from another widget, copy it into src/ directly. Examples must run and exit cleanly with no user input, no network calls, and no external services or API keys - use fake/hardcoded data. The widget's own declared dependencies are fine and will be installed by the validator before the example runs. Do not modify library_notes - it is managed by Cartograph and will be restored on checkin.
library_notes.language: No project-specific names, identifiers, or terminology anywhere in src/, tests/, or examples/ - use generic names like 'item', 'value', 'exampleUser'. Use std/unittest for all tests (import std/unittest, not bare import unittest). Use func for pure procedures with no side effects — only use proc when side effects are required. Mark all public symbols with * (e.g. func myFunc*(x: int): int). Use camelCase for funcs/procs/variables, PascalCase for types. Prefer stdlib imports with the std/ prefix (e.g. import std/strutils, not import strutils). No echo in src/ files. No quit() or system.quit() in src/ files. No {.importc.} or {.compile.} pragmas — C FFI makes widgets platform-dependent and unvalidatable. No {.global.} pragma — widgets must not use global mutable state. No OS-targeting when defined(windows/linux/posix/etc.) — widgets must validate on all platforms. A widget_id.nimble file is required at the widget root - it is created by cartograph create and must stay in sync with widget.json dependencies. List nimble package dependencies in widget.json tech_stack.dependencies as 'pkgname >= version'.
library_notes.domain: Use this domain when a widget spans multiple domains or has no clear home — not because it must be pure. A retry utility, a date formatter, a general-purpose HTTP client all belong here. Expose variation points as parameters. Don't force it into a domain just to avoid universal.
